title: Velvety Sour Canadian Beef Bisque
description: A rich beef soup originating from Canada, made with a bain-marie to create a smooth and velvety texture, served as a satisfying post-workout meal.
instructions: In a large saucepan, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the onion and garlic to the saucepan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the flour to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth to the saucepan, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth, or allow it to cool and puree it in a blender.
Return the soup to the saucepan and add the heavy cream, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper. Simmer over low heat for 5 minutes.
Stir in the ground clove and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the bisque hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
Use a bain-marie to keep the soup warm for a longer period of time.
